sections.heading: What is Gson?
sections.content: Gson is a JSON parsing library developed by Google that converts Kotlin/Java objects into JSON (serialization) and JSON back into objects (deserialization). In Android development, Gson is commonly used with Retrofit to automatically convert API responses into Kotlin data classes. Instead of manually reading JSON fields, Gson maps JSON keys to object properties using reflection.

sections.heading: Why Use Gson?
items: Simple and widely used JSON parsing library
Works automatically with Retrofit using GsonConverterFactory
Requires minimal configuration for basic parsing
Supports nested objects, lists, and complex structures
Supports annotations like @SerializedName for custom mapping

sections.heading: Basic Gson Usage
sections.content: A Gson instance is used to convert JSON strings into Kotlin objects and vice versa. Deserialization converts JSON into objects using fromJson(), while serialization converts objects into JSON using toJson().

sections.heading: Core Gson Operations
items: Convert JSON to object: gson.fromJson(jsonString, User::class.java)
Convert JSON array to list using TypeToken for generic types
Use GsonBuilder() when custom configuration is needed

rows.description: JSON number maps to Kotlin numeric types
JSON string maps to Kotlin String
JSON array maps to Kotlin List
Nested JSON objects map to nested data classes

sections.heading: @SerializedName Annotation
sections.content: Many APIs use snake_case JSON keys while Kotlin uses camelCase property names. The @SerializedName annotation allows Gson to map JSON keys to different Kotlin property names.

sections.heading: Common @SerializedName Usage
items: Allows Kotlin naming conventions while matching API responses

sections.heading: Gson with Retrofit
sections.content: Retrofit integrates directly with Gson using GsonConverterFactory. Once added to Retrofit's builder, Gson automatically converts API responses into Kotlin data classes and converts request bodies into JSON. This removes the need to manually call fromJson() or toJson() when making API requests.

sections.heading: Retrofit Integration Steps
items: Define API interfaces returning Kotlin data classes
Retrofit automatically parses JSON responses using Gson

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Gson is a popular JSON parsing library in Android that converts JSON data into Kotlin objects and back. It integrates seamlessly with Retrofit, allowing API responses to be automatically mapped to data classes. Features such as @SerializedName help handle differences between JSON key naming and Kotlin property naming, making networking code cleaner and easier to maintain.
